Register to take classes for Role of Men (ROM) Workshop
Role Of Men is open to men of all races who are fathers, expectant fathers, or men contemplating fatherhood. ROM provides 6-week workshops, on a father’s role in parenting. The workshops recur throughout the year. These workshops provide men with resources and encourage increased participation in parenting.
If interested, use the following link to register:
Register for classes here
The class curriculum is based on 24/7 Dad program, developed by “The National Fatherhood Initiative” with a focus on being positive role models.
Participants will receive one-on-one meetings, workshops (virtual or in-person), as well as assistance in accessing resources.
Curriculum topics include:
Character of a man
Discipline
Co-parenting
Children’s growth
Improving communication
Handling and showing feelings
Healthy relationships
Fatherhood self-assessment
Family history
Developing a support system

Role of Men program
Robert Wilson, Role of Men Coordinator explains how he creates a safe space for men to grow. Role of Men is open to men of all races who are fathers, expectant fathers, men contemplating fatherhood or men looking to improve themselves and their relationships. These workshops provide men with resources and encourage increased participation in parenting.
